name: Nori-Maki Sushi
recipeIngredient: 8 Nori sheets (use pretoasted nori, especially made for making sushi)
Cucumber - peeled, seeded, and quartered lengthwise
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the rice in a bowl of cold water. Swish around with your hands and drain when the water becomes cloudy. Fill with more water and repeat until the water remains very clear.
2. Place the rice in a heavy 4-quart saucepan. Add the water and cover with the lid. Bring to a boil without lifting the lid (watch for the lid jiggling). Reduce heat to medium high and cook for 5 minutes, listening for a hissing or crackling sound indicating the water is absorbed. Remove from heat and let stand for 20-30 minutes.
3. Transfer rice to a large bowl. Toss with a Japanese paddle or wooden spoon until it stops steaming.
4. In a small bowl, combine vinegar, sugar, and salt. Stir until sugar dissolves. Sprinkle into the rice, a little at a time, and toss. Cover with a clean tea towel and set aside.
5. Prepare wasabi paste by mixing about 1 tablespoon of wasabi powder with enough water to make a thin mixture.
6. Place a sheet of nori, shiny side down, on a bamboo mat. Spread about 3/4 cup vinegared rice over the nori, leaving a 1/2 inch border on the ends.
7. Brush the side closest to you with wasabi paste (use sparingly). Arrange vegetable strips (6 or so) across the rice.
8. Lift the mat edge and roll tightly until sealed. Repeat with remaining nori sheets.
9. Cut rolls into six pieces using a wet, sharp knife. Arrange on a platter.
10. Mix dipping sauce ingredients in a bowl. Divide among six small bowls for guests to dip rolls.
Vegetarian Celebrations by Nava Atlas
